productVision: Create a trusted AI-native operating system for real property that turns fragmented maintenance, service delivery, and community governance into a continuous, evidence-backed digital experience. Hestami's north star is persistent property context that improves decisions, coordination, compliance, and outcomes across owners, providers, staff, and associations.
productDescription: Hestami AI Real Property OS and Platform is a multi-sided real property operations platform that unifies three connected pillars: owner and portfolio property context management with AI-assisted intake and service coordination, contractor and field-service execution from dispatch through billing, and community association operations covering requests, governance, accounting, compliance, and resident portal experiences. Across these pillars, the platform centralizes property records, party relationships, documents, communications, workflows, approvals, financial events, and decision evidence so work can move from issue detection to resolution with greater trust, traceability, and operational control. Its differentiator is the use of persistent digital property context and a decision-and-evidence ledger to improve provider matching, service execution, stakeholder coordination, and governance quality across the full property lifecycle.
summary: Hestami is positioned as a unified operating system for real property operations, anchored in persistent property context and AI-assisted coordination. It serves a broad network of users that includes homeowners, landlords, investors, service providers, field technicians, association managers, boards, management companies, residents, tenants, finance staff, compliance coordinators, and Hestami's own operating staff. The settled product shape centers on owner and portfolio record management, AI-assisted issue intake, service-case orchestration, provider discovery and trust, field-service operations, association operations, governance workflows, accounting traceability, and role-based portal experiences. A core through-line across the platform is durable operational memory: documents, communications, approvals, evidence, and rationale are preserved so maintenance, compliance, and decisions remain auditable over time. The near-term emphasis remains the owner-side and concierge-assisted service experience, where rich property context improves problem intake, provider selection, and execution quality. Later phases expand more fully into contractor operating systems and comprehensive community association management, while keeping the three pillars connected through shared records, workflows, and integrations.

phasingStrategy.description: Deliver the core owner-side value proposition: persistent property context, service-call intake, provider discovery and matching support, human-assisted coordination, external HOA context tracking, and a durable decision/evidence record.
phasingStrategy.rationale: This phase addresses the most explicit and urgent user pain in the spec: owners waste time and incur risk when trying to get property work done. It creates the foundational property and case data needed for the broader platform while allowing Hestami to provide near-term concierge-style value without requiring full provider or HOA platform adoption.

phasingStrategy.description: Expand into service-provider operational software so matched providers and contractor businesses can run dispatch, field execution, estimating, invoicing, and recurring service inside the platform.
phasingStrategy.rationale: After owner-side demand generation and coordination are in place, contractor-side tools deepen marketplace liquidity, improve execution quality, and create tighter operational integration between requests and fulfilled jobs.

phasingStrategy.description: Add community association management capabilities including association records, accounting-adjacent workflows, communications, governance, owner portals, ARC, violations, and compliance operations.
phasingStrategy.rationale: The CAM pillar is broad and operationally dense. It adds major strategic expansion value by connecting property owners and communities into the same operating system, but it is less essential than Phase 1 for proving the initial owner-service use case.

successMetrics: Property owners can create a property record and associated service call without needing off-platform coordination for basic intake
Time from owner request creation to provider shortlist or match is materially reduced versus manual search
A meaningful percentage of service calls include reusable property context such as media, documents, or historical records
Owners can track case status and outcome from intake through closure within a persistent case record
Matched or coordinated service requests convert into completed outcomes at a measurable rate
For Phase 2, service providers can manage jobs from request through invoice inside the platform
For Phase 3, community managers, board members, and owners can complete role-based association workflows with auditable records preserved

businessDomainProposals.name: Property Context and Digital Record
businessDomainProposals.description: The persistent system of record for a home or real property, including address, location, structural details, spaces, systems, photos, videos, and evolving property metadata.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: This is foundational to the product vision of a persistent property context and is directly required by UJ-1 and Phase 1 property onboarding. It anchors owner workflows, service execution, documents, HOA context, and long-term history across all three pillars.

businessDomainProposals.name: Ownership, Tenancy, and Party Relationships
businessDomainProposals.description: Modeling of people and organizations connected to a property or association, including owners, residents, tenants, boards, managers, vendors, and their authorized relationships and access boundaries.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly call for a common data model spanning people, ownership or tenancy relationships, and owner portal roles. This domain is necessary for portfolio views, association self-service, tenant access, board governance, and vendor interaction.

businessDomainProposals.name: Portfolio and Multi-Property Management
businessDomainProposals.description: Tools for organizing and operating multiple homes, units, or associations under a single owner, investor, management company, or operator context.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: UJ-7 directly establishes the need for managing multiple properties, and the discovered landlord, investor, and management-company personas depend on cross-property visibility. This extends the single-property record into scalable operations.

businessDomainProposals.name: Owner and Resident Intent Intake
businessDomainProposals.description: Structured capture of what a user is trying to accomplish, including maintenance requests, repair issues, installations, questions, and related context gathered through AI-assisted intake.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly call out owner intent intake, and UJ-2 begins with a homeowner needing to create a structured service case. This domain is essential for turning unstructured problems into actionable workflows.

businessDomainProposals.name: Service Call and Work Lifecycle
businessDomainProposals.description: End-to-end management of service cases and work orders from intake through triage, quoting, scheduling, execution, completion, follow-up, and closure.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Service call lifecycle is explicitly named in the requirements and is central to UJ-2, UJ-4, UJ-8, UJ-9, and CAM work-order requirements. It bridges owner-side requests, provider operations, and association maintenance workflows.

businessDomainProposals.name: Service Provider Discovery, Matching, and Trust
businessDomainProposals.description: Discovery, evaluation, matching, and trust-building workflows for selecting service providers, including internet-assisted search, trade matching, qualifications, and fit assessment.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: UJ-3 and the requirements explicitly state that AI agents or Hestami staff facilitate provider discovery and matching. This domain addresses the strongest owner pain points around trust, legitimacy, and finding the right contractor.

businessDomainProposals.name: Vendor Identity, Licensing, Insurance, and Compliance
businessDomainProposals.description: Management of service-provider business identity and compliance artifacts such as licenses, insurance, certifications, service areas, and operating credentials.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The product requirements explicitly include contractor identity and compliance, and homeowner pain points emphasize difficulty verifying legitimacy, licensing, and insurance. This domain also supports CAM vendor oversight and auditability.

businessDomainProposals.name: Bids, Estimates, Pricing, Invoicing, and Payments
businessDomainProposals.description: Commercial workflows for generating proposals, pricebook-based estimates, invoices, on-site payments, and financial transitions from approved work to payable or receivable states.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The product description and requirements reference comparing bids, producing estimates and invoices, invoice-to-AP workflows, and technician payment acceptance. This domain is necessary across homeowner, provider, and association maintenance flows.

businessDomainProposals.name: Scheduling, Dispatch, and Field Coordination
businessDomainProposals.description: Coordination of appointments, calendars, technician assignment, route-aware dispatch, access windows, follow-up visits, and schedule changes.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: UJ-4, UJ-8, and UJ-9 all depend on effective scheduling and dispatch, and the FSM requirements explicitly include dispatch and scheduling. This domain reduces one of the primary friction points for owners and providers alike.

businessDomainProposals.name: Technician Mobile Operations
businessDomainProposals.description: Mobile-first field execution for technicians, including job details, documentation capture, time tracking, estimate generation, signatures, payment collection, and offline usage.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Technician mobile operations are explicitly required and map directly to UJ-9. This domain is distinct from dispatch because it focuses on what happens on site during execution and proof of work.

businessDomainProposals.name: Documents, Records, and AI Extraction
businessDomainProposals.description: Centralized storage and management of property, service, association, legal, and financial documents with AI-driven extraction, classification, summarization, and retrieval.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Property document management is explicitly required, and the broader platform must support governing documents, contracts, inspection reports, reserve studies, ARC plans, and financial records. UJ-5 and UJ-6 also depend on trustworthy document access.
workflowPreview: Retrieve Related Records for a Case

businessDomainProposals.name: Communications, Notifications, and Messaging
businessDomainProposals.description: Cross-channel communications for messages, SMS, email, announcements, notices, letters, calendar events, and meeting-related notifications tied to operational workflows.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly call for mass email, SMS, announcements, letter templates, calendar events, and meeting notices. This domain supports coordination across owners, residents, providers, managers, boards, and staff.

businessDomainProposals.name: Workflow Automation, Tasks, Approvals, and SLA Control
businessDomainProposals.description: Automation infrastructure for tasks, triggers, approval routing, SLA timers, escalations, reminders, and status-driven process orchestration.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly call for workflow automation with tasks, triggers, SLA timers, notifications, and approvals. This domain underpins case handling, board approvals, ARC review, notices, and compliance deadlines across all pillars.

businessDomainProposals.name: Decision, Evidence, and Rationale Ledger
businessDomainProposals.description: A durable audit trail of decisions, supporting evidence, rationale, approvals, and outcomes for property, service, financial, and governance actions.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly name a decision or rationale trust ledger, and UJ-6 is centered on maintaining a trustable decision and evidence history. This domain is core to the platform's promise of reduced risk and greater transparency.

businessDomainProposals.name: External HOA and Property Governance Context
businessDomainProposals.description: Tracking of HOA, association, permit, approval, and rule context relevant to a property even before the association itself is fully onboarded to the platform.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: External HOA context tracking is explicitly required for Phase 1 and maps directly to UJ-5. It helps owners understand governing constraints before work starts and prepares a bridge into the CAM pillar.

businessDomainProposals.name: Community Association Operations and Requests
businessDomainProposals.description: Operational management for associations, including work orders, owner requests, vendor coordination, community tasks, and day-to-day action tracking.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: UJ-10 requires one system for community operations, and the CAM pillar requirements explicitly include work orders, vendor management, and operational workflows. This is the execution layer for managers and staff.
workflowPreview: Coordinate Vendor for Common Area Issue

businessDomainProposals.name: Association Accounting and Financial Operations
businessDomainProposals.description: Auditable accounting capabilities for associations, including general ledger structure, assessment posting, accounts payable, payment flows, fines, and financial reporting support.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly call for compliant general ledger capabilities, automatic periodic assessments, and integration from work orders to AP to payments. This domain is central to the CAM pillar and financial trust.

businessDomainProposals.name: Board Governance, Meetings, and Voting
businessDomainProposals.description: Support for boards, board roles, agendas, minutes, resolutions, meetings, policies, secure review, and formal voting or approval processes.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: UJ-11 and the governance requirements explicitly call for boards, meetings, agendas, minutes, voting, resolutions, and policy documents. This domain serves board members, managers, and management companies that need defensible governance workflows.

businessDomainProposals.name: Owner, Resident, Tenant, and Vendor Portal Experience
businessDomainProposals.description: Self-service portals and CRM-style access for owners, residents, tenants, vendors, and managers with role-based permissions for requests, payments, documents, and communications.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly call for role-based owner portal capabilities spanning owner, tenant, manager, and vendor. UJ-12 depends on self-service access, and this domain connects all personas to the system in a role-appropriate way.

businessDomainProposals.name: Architectural Review, Violations, and Compliance
businessDomainProposals.description: Governed workflows for ARC submissions, plan review, conditions, permits, violations, evidence, notice sequences, hearings, fines, and statutory compliance requirements.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: UJ-13 directly covers ARC and compliance workflows, and the requirements explicitly specify notices, hearings, fine linkage, permit context, deadlines, voting rules, audits, and resale packet guidelines. This is a major CAM operating domain with high legal and procedural sensitivity.

businessDomainProposals.name: Asset, Inventory, Materials, and Maintenance Programs
businessDomainProposals.description: Management of physical assets, spare materials, inventory, procurement, recurring maintenance plans, and maintenance contracts across homes, vendor operations, and associations.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The requirements explicitly include assets, pricebook and materials, inventory and procurement, recurring service, and maintenance contracts. This domain is standard for field service and association maintenance operations and supports preventive rather than purely reactive work.

businessDomainProposals.name: Reserve Studies and Capital Planning
businessDomainProposals.description: Storage, analysis, and operational use of reserve studies, component lifecycles, funding projections, and major repair or replacement planning for associations.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Reserve studies are explicitly included in the common data model and CAM requirements. This domain is especially relevant for boards, managers, and management companies making long-range maintenance and budget decisions.

businessDomainProposals.name: Internal Staff, Concierge, and Marketplace Operations
businessDomainProposals.description: Internal Hestami operational tooling for staff-assisted service coordination, case oversight, quality control, intervention management, and marketplace health.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: Human staff execution is explicitly one of the Phase 1 business domains, and UJ-3 and UJ-4 both rely on Hestami staff or AI agents actively coordinating outcomes. This domain gives internal teams the controls needed to operate the service layer.

businessDomainProposals.name: External Integrations and Ecosystem Connectivity
businessDomainProposals.description: Connections to internet search, payments, accounting systems, maps, communications providers, document sources, and other third-party systems required to complete workflows.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The platform explicitly depends on internet-assisted provider discovery and payment or accounting-adjacent workflows. In a property operations product, integration capability is a standard cross-cutting domain needed to avoid data silos and manual re-entry.

businessDomainProposals.name: Operational Analytics, Reporting, and Performance Insights
businessDomainProposals.description: Dashboards, KPIs, audit reports, portfolio views, and performance analysis for owners, providers, managers, and internal teams.
businessDomainProposals.rationale: The product vision emphasizes lower risk, greater transparency, and portfolio-wide visibility, especially for investors, management companies, and boards. Reporting is standard for this industry and becomes necessary once the platform centralizes operational and financial records.

entityProposals.name: Property
entityProposals.description: The primary system-of-record entity for a home, unit, building, lot, or managed real property.

entityProposals.name: Address
entityProposals.description: Normalized physical or mailing address associated with a property, party, vendor, or association.

entityProposals.name: Property Geopoint
entityProposals.description: Latitude, longitude, and geospatial metadata used for mapping, service area matching, and routing.

entityProposals.name: Property Space
entityProposals.description: A named physical space or area within a property such as kitchen, roof, garage, unit, or common area.

entityProposals.name: Property System
entityProposals.description: A major building system such as HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, or appliance grouping tracked within a property record.

entityProposals.name: Property Media Asset
entityProposals.description: A photo, video, or visual record tied to a property, space, system, or issue history.

entityProposals.name: Property Metadata Snapshot
entityProposals.description: Versioned snapshot of important property facts over time for history and comparison.

entityProposals.name: Party
entityProposals.description: A person or organization connected to a property, association, case, or financial workflow.

entityProposals.name: Party Relationship
entityProposals.description: A modeled relationship between a party and a property, unit, association, portfolio, or another party with effective dates and boundaries.

entityProposals.name: Access Grant
entityProposals.description: Role-based permission assignment defining what a party can view or do within a scoped resource.

entityProposals.name: Access Grant Rule
entityProposals.description: Fine-grained permission rule or restriction attached to an access grant.

entityProposals.name: Communication Endpoint
entityProposals.description: A verified email address, phone number, mailing address, or channel handle used for communications with a party.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio
entityProposals.description: An owner, investor, operator, or management grouping that aggregates multiple properties or associations.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio Property
entityProposals.description: Junction entity linking a portfolio to a property with ownership or management context.

entityProposals.name: Portfolio Association
entityProposals.description: Junction entity linking a portfolio or management company context to an association.

entityProposals.name: Intent Intake
entityProposals.description: Structured capture of a user-reported issue, request, or question derived from free-form input.

entityProposals.name: Intake Artifact
entityProposals.description: Supporting note, transcript, photo, media item, extracted fact, or prompt output captured during intake.

entityProposals.name: Triage Assessment
entityProposals.description: Structured triage output including urgency, likely trade, safety signals, and routing recommendations.

entityProposals.name: Service Case
entityProposals.description: The central operational case that manages a maintenance or service issue from intake through closure.

entityProposals.name: Case Participant
entityProposals.description: Junction entity representing parties involved in a service case such as owner, tenant, manager, vendor, or concierge.

entityProposals.name: Work Order
entityProposals.description: A unit of executable work within a service case, often assigned to a provider or technician team.

entityProposals.name: Case Status History
entityProposals.description: Audit trail of service case state transitions with timestamps, actors, and reasons.

entityProposals.name: Provider Profile
entityProposals.description: Marketplace-facing service provider profile used for discovery, fit evaluation, and trust presentation.

entityProposals.name: Provider Trade
entityProposals.description: Trade, specialty, or service category a provider offers.

entityProposals.name: Provider Service Area
entityProposals.description: Geographic coverage area where a provider is willing to perform work.

entityProposals.name: Provider Match Candidate
entityProposals.description: A candidate provider considered for a service case, including fit scores and match rationale.

entityProposals.name: Match Rationale Factor
entityProposals.description: Specific signal contributing to a provider match outcome such as distance, trade fit, rating, or compliance status.

entityProposals.name: Provider Trust Signal
entityProposals.description: A trust-oriented signal such as rating, review summary, years in business, response rate, or verification badge.

entityProposals.name: Vendor Credential
entityProposals.description: A compliance artifact such as license, insurance policy, certification, bond, or registration held by a provider.

entityProposals.name: Credential Document
entityProposals.description: Documentary evidence attached to a vendor credential, including uploaded proofs and extracted metadata.

entityProposals.name: Compliance Check
entityProposals.description: Verification event assessing whether a provider meets licensing, insurance, and policy requirements for a case or marketplace participation.

entityProposals.name: Compliance Check Finding
entityProposals.description: Individual pass, fail, warning, or exception result produced during a compliance verification.

entityProposals.name: Estimate
entityProposals.description: A provider-generated or platform-generated estimate describing proposed scope, pricing, and assumptions for work.

entityProposals.name: Estimate Line Item
entityProposals.description: A priced line item within an estimate for labor, materials, fees, allowances, or discounts.

entityProposals.name: Pricebook Item
entityProposals.description: Reusable catalog item for standard service, labor, or material pricing.

entityProposals.name: Invoice
entityProposals.description: A bill issued for completed or billable work tied to a work order, case, or association charge event.

entityProposals.name: Invoice Line Item
entityProposals.description: A charge or credit line on an invoice.

entityProposals.name: Payment
entityProposals.description: A payment transaction received from or sent to a party in relation to invoices, assessments, fines, or payables.

entityProposals.name: Payment Allocation
entityProposals.description: Allocation of a payment amount to a specific invoice, charge, fine, or ledger item.

entityProposals.name: Appointment
entityProposals.description: A scheduled visit, estimate slot, inspection, or service appointment tied to a work order or case.

entityProposals.name: Dispatch Assignment
entityProposals.description: Assignment of a technician or crew to an appointment with dispatch and routing details.

entityProposals.name: Access Window
entityProposals.description: A permitted or preferred property access window, lockbox instruction set, or gate constraint for a scheduled visit.

entityProposals.name: Field Work Session
entityProposals.description: A technician's on-site or in-transit execution session for a work order including start, stop, and sync state.

entityProposals.name: Time Entry
entityProposals.description: Tracked labor time associated with a field work session or work order.

entityProposals.name: Field Artifact
entityProposals.description: On-site note, checklist response, photo, video, reading, or completion proof captured by a technician.

entityProposals.name: Signature Record
entityProposals.description: A captured signature for approval, change authorization, completion acknowledgment, or delivery of service.

entityProposals.name: Document Record
entityProposals.description: A centrally stored document or file such as PDF, image, video, statement, policy, permit, or contract.

entityProposals.name: Document Link
entityProposals.description: Polymorphic link connecting a stored document to a business entity such as property, case, invoice, credential, or meeting.

entityProposals.name: Extraction Result
entityProposals.description: AI-generated structured extraction, summary, or classification output derived from a document.

entityProposals.name: Extracted Field
entityProposals.description: Individual extracted data point from a document with confidence and provenance.

entityProposals.name: Message Thread
entityProposals.description: Conversation thread spanning one or more parties and one or more channels around a case, request, or community topic.

entityProposals.name: Thread Participant
entityProposals.description: Junction entity linking a party to a message thread with participation role and visibility settings.

entityProposals.name: Message
entityProposals.description: A single outbound or inbound communication event sent via email, SMS, in-app, letter, or other channel.

entityProposals.name: Notification Delivery
entityProposals.description: Delivery attempt and receipt status for a message to a specific endpoint.

entityProposals.name: Workflow Task
entityProposals.description: Action item generated manually or automatically within operational workflows.

entityProposals.name: Task Assignment
entityProposals.description: Assignment of a workflow task to a party, queue, or team.

entityProposals.name: Approval Request
entityProposals.description: Structured request for one or more approvers to review and approve a workflow action or decision.

entityProposals.name: Approval Response
entityProposals.description: Decision record from an approver for an approval request.

entityProposals.name: SLA Policy
entityProposals.description: Configurable service-level timing rules for tasks, responses, notices, approvals, or operational workflows.

entityProposals.name: SLA Timer
entityProposals.description: Runtime timer tracking elapsed time against an SLA policy for a specific workflow instance.

entityProposals.name: Decision Ledger Entry
entityProposals.description: Durable ledger record of a decision, rationale, evidence set, and outcome.

entityProposals.name: Decision Evidence Link
entityProposals.description: Link from a decision ledger entry to supporting evidence such as documents, media, comparisons, or rule references.

entityProposals.name: Decision Participant
entityProposals.description: Party involved in a recorded decision as approver, recommender, reviewer, or affected party.

entityProposals.name: Association
entityProposals.description: An HOA, condo association, or community governance body relevant to one or more properties.

entityProposals.name: Association Property
entityProposals.description: Junction linking a property or unit to an association with membership or jurisdiction context.

entityProposals.name: HOA Rule
entityProposals.description: Tracked association rule, covenant, policy, or requirement relevant to property actions and approvals.

entityProposals.name: Permit Requirement
entityProposals.description: Municipal or association-linked permit or approval requirement relevant to a property scope of work.

entityProposals.name: Association Request
entityProposals.description: Resident, owner, or staff-submitted operational request for a community or association.

entityProposals.name: Community Task
entityProposals.description: Operational action item for common-area management, resident follow-up, vendor coordination, or administrative work.

entityProposals.name: GL Account
entityProposals.description: General ledger account used for association accounting classification and reporting.

entityProposals.name: Financial Ledger Entry
entityProposals.description: Accounting journal or ledger entry representing a financial event for an association.

entityProposals.name: Assessment Charge
entityProposals.description: Assessment or dues charge posted to an owner account within an association.

entityProposals.name: Owner Account Balance
entityProposals.description: Running balance summary for an owner or unit account within an association.

entityProposals.name: AP Bill
entityProposals.description: Accounts payable bill owed by the association to a vendor or provider.

entityProposals.name: Meeting
entityProposals.description: A scheduled board, committee, or governance meeting for an association.

entityProposals.name: Agenda Item
entityProposals.description: A structured agenda topic or action item within a meeting.

entityProposals.name: Meeting Participant
entityProposals.description: Party attendance and role record for a governance meeting.

entityProposals.name: Resolution
entityProposals.description: Formal board resolution, policy action, or ratified decision subject to review and recordkeeping.

entityProposals.name: Vote
entityProposals.description: Recorded vote on a resolution, agenda item, or governance action.

entityProposals.name: Vote Ballot
entityProposals.description: Individual ballot or recorded vote cast by a party in a governance vote.

entityProposals.name: Portal Account
entityProposals.description: Self-service portal identity and account wrapper used by owners, residents, tenants, vendors, and managers.

entityProposals.name: Portal Session
entityProposals.description: Authenticated portal session used for audit, security, and experience continuity.

entityProposals.name: Portal Subscription
entityProposals.description: Portal preference record controlling what resources, alerts, or dashboard widgets a user follows.

entityProposals.name: ARC Case
entityProposals.description: Architectural review submission or compliance case tracking plans, reviews, conditions, notices, and outcomes.

entityProposals.name: ARC Submission
entityProposals.description: Submitted package of plans, documents, and metadata for architectural review.

entityProposals.name: Violation Notice
entityProposals.description: Formal notice issued in a violation or compliance procedure, including notice type and service status.

entityProposals.name: Hearing
entityProposals.description: Scheduled compliance or violation hearing tied to a case.

entityProposals.name: Fine Assessment
entityProposals.description: Financial penalty assessed in connection with a violation or enforcement outcome.

entityProposals.name: Asset
entityProposals.description: Tracked physical asset, component, equipment item, or maintainable object within a property or association.

entityProposals.name: Maintenance Plan
entityProposals.description: Recurring maintenance program defining cadence, procedures, and service expectations for an asset or property.

entityProposals.name: Maintenance Task
entityProposals.description: Scheduled or generated maintenance task under a recurring plan.

entityProposals.name: Inventory Item
entityProposals.description: Tracked material, spare part, or stocked item used in service or association maintenance operations.

entityProposals.name: Inventory Transaction
entityProposals.description: Movement of inventory into, out of, or between locations, jobs, or stock ownership contexts.

entityProposals.name: Reserve Study
entityProposals.description: A reserve study document and data model for forecasting major repairs, replacements, and funding needs.

entityProposals.name: Reserve Component
entityProposals.description: A repair or replacement component tracked within a reserve study such as roof, paving, or mechanical system.

entityProposals.name: Funding Projection
entityProposals.description: Projected timing and funding requirement for a reserve component or capital plan scenario.

entityProposals.name: Staff Intervention
entityProposals.description: Internal Hestami action taken to supervise, unblock, correct, or escalate a customer, provider, or marketplace workflow.

entityProposals.name: Quality Review
entityProposals.description: Internal review of workflow quality, provider performance, or case handling outcomes.

entityProposals.name: Marketplace Health Metric
entityProposals.description: Operational marketplace metric tracked for coverage, responsiveness, quality, or trust health.

entityProposals.name: Integration Connection
entityProposals.description: Configured connection to an external system such as payments, accounting, communications, maps, or document source.

entityProposals.name: External System Link
entityProposals.description: Mapping between an internal entity and its identifier or object in an external system.

entityProposals.name: Integration Sync Run
entityProposals.description: Execution record for an import, export, webhook handling event, or scheduled synchronization job.

entityProposals.name: Analytic Metric Definition
entityProposals.description: Definition of a KPI or calculated metric used in dashboards and reports.

entityProposals.name: Analytic Snapshot
entityProposals.description: Computed metric value or dimensional aggregate captured for a reporting period and scope.

entityProposals.name: Report Definition
entityProposals.description: Saved report, dashboard, or export configuration for operational and financial visibility.

entityProposals.name: Report Run
entityProposals.description: Execution record and output metadata for a generated report or dashboard snapshot.

workflowProposals.name: Property record creation and enrichment
workflowProposals.description: Builds and maintains the persistent system of record for a property.
steps: Create a new property shell from address and ownership context
Capture structured property attributes, spaces, systems, and baseline condition data
Attach media, documents, and extracted metadata to the property record
Update the record over time from service events and new uploads

workflowProposals.name: Party relationship and permission assignment
workflowProposals.description: Manages who is connected to a property or association and what they are allowed to do.
steps: Create person and organization profiles
Assign relationship type, effective dates, and authority scope
Apply access controls to records, requests, communications, and payments
Revoke or modify access when relationships change
triggers: New owner, tenant, board member, vendor, or manager added

workflowProposals.name: Multi-property portfolio aggregation
workflowProposals.description: Aggregates properties, communities, and operational activity into a portfolio view.
steps: Group properties or associations under a portfolio owner or operator
Normalize status, activity, and financial signals across entities
Surface exceptions, trends, and missing records
Support filtering and drill-down to underlying property or association details

workflowProposals.name: AI-assisted issue and request intake
workflowProposals.description: Converts a free-form request into structured intent and triage-ready context.
steps: Capture a user description, media, urgency, and location context
Ask clarifying questions based on the stated issue
Classify intent, likely trade, urgency, and affected property area
Create a structured intake package for downstream workflows
triggers: New owner, tenant, resident, or staff request
Inbound message converted into a request

workflowProposals.name: Service case orchestration
workflowProposals.description: Moves service work from intake through triage, coordination, execution, and closure.
steps: Open a service case from structured intake
Attach property context, documents, communications, and provider interactions
Advance the case through triage, decision, scheduling, execution, and closure states
Store final outcome and follow-up requirements

workflowProposals.name: Provider search and matching
workflowProposals.description: Finds candidate providers that fit the trade, geography, urgency, and trust requirements of a case.
steps: Derive provider requirements from the intake and property context
Search internal and external provider sources
Score candidates by trade fit, service area, responsiveness, and trust indicators
Return a shortlist for staff or owner review

workflowProposals.name: Provider compliance verification
workflowProposals.description: Tracks business identity, licensing, insurance, and related credentials for providers.
steps: Collect provider business profile and compliance artifacts
Validate dates, jurisdictions, and coverage status
Flag missing, expired, or mismatched credentials
Expose compliance status to matching, scheduling, and association workflows

workflowProposals.name: Estimate, invoice, and payment processing
workflowProposals.description: Supports pricing, proposal, invoicing, and payment transitions tied to work.
steps: Generate estimate or bid from scope and price inputs
Submit for review or customer approval
Convert approved work into invoiceable activity
Record invoice issuance, payment, and status changes

workflowProposals.name: Appointment scheduling and dispatch
workflowProposals.description: Coordinates calendars, technician assignments, and appointment changes.
steps: Offer or select appointment windows
Assign technician or crew based on skill and availability
Send confirmations, reminders, and updates
Handle reschedules, delays, and follow-up visits

workflowProposals.name: Mobile field execution and sync
workflowProposals.description: Supports technicians on site with documentation, approvals, and completion workflows.
steps: Deliver job packet to the mobile device
Capture arrival, diagnostics, notes, media, and time on site
Support offline entry and later synchronization
Record completion proof, signatures, and optional payment

workflowProposals.name: Document ingestion and AI extraction
workflowProposals.description: Stores documents centrally and turns them into usable structured records.
steps: Ingest uploaded or imported documents
Classify document type and associate it to the right property, case, or association
Extract key entities, dates, and summaries
Index the document for retrieval and downstream use

workflowProposals.name: Cross-channel workflow communications
workflowProposals.description: Sends and records messages, notices, and workflow-driven notifications.
steps: Generate communication payload from workflow state or manual action
Select channel and recipients based on permissions and urgency
Deliver message and record delivery state
Associate message history with the relevant property, case, or association record

workflowProposals.name: Task, approval, and SLA automation
workflowProposals.description: Creates and advances tasks, approvals, reminders, and escalations based on workflow logic.
steps: Watch for configured triggers such as status changes or deadlines
Create tasks, approval requests, or reminders
Escalate overdue or blocked items according to SLA rules
Close automation items when underlying work is completed

workflowProposals.name: Decision and evidence logging
workflowProposals.description: Creates a durable record of decisions, supporting evidence, and outcomes.
steps: Capture a decision event from service, financial, or governance workflows
Attach supporting evidence, documents, and rationale
Record participants, timestamps, and resulting actions
Preserve an auditable immutable-style history for later review

workflowProposals.name: External HOA and rule context tracking
workflowProposals.description: Tracks association rules, approval needs, and governance context relevant to a property before full association onboarding.
steps: Capture HOA identity, rules, contacts, and known approval requirements
Link the context to affected properties and work types
Surface rule implications during owner service workflows
Update the context when new documents or guidance are obtained
triggers: Property onboarded into an HOA-governed context

workflowProposals.name: Association request and work management
workflowProposals.description: Handles community operational requests, work orders, and follow-through.
steps: Open an association operational item from a portal request or internal need
Assign responsibility, vendor tasks, and internal follow-up
Track status, resident communication, and supporting records
Close the item with final disposition and retained history

workflowProposals.name: Association accounting event processing
workflowProposals.description: Processes assessments, payables, owner balances, and financial controls for associations.
steps: Post recurring assessments and owner charges
Record payments, payables, credits, and fines
Link transactions to operational or compliance source events when applicable
Support reconciliation and reporting outputs

workflowProposals.name: Meeting, agenda, and voting administration
workflowProposals.description: Supports formal board governance processes from agenda preparation through recorded decisions.
steps: Prepare meeting or approval packet with agenda and supporting materials
Distribute materials to authorized participants
Record attendance, discussion outcomes, and votes
Store minutes, resolutions, and follow-up actions

workflowProposals.name: Role-based portal interaction handling
workflowProposals.description: Provides self-service views and actions for owners, residents, tenants, and vendors.
steps: Authenticate the user and resolve their relationship context
Display role-appropriate dashboard data, records, and available actions
Accept portal submissions such as payments, requests, or document views
Feed actions back into operational workflows and history

workflowProposals.name: ARC and violation procedure enforcement
workflowProposals.description: Executes policy-driven review, notice, hearing, and enforcement workflows.
steps: Open an ARC or violation case with governing rule context
Generate required deadlines, notices, and review steps
Collect evidence, decisions, and hearing outcomes
Apply final disposition, conditions, and any linked penalties

workflowProposals.name: Asset and recurring maintenance program management
workflowProposals.description: Tracks physical assets, maintenance plans, materials, and recurring service requirements.
steps: Create asset records and maintenance schedules
Track service intervals, materials, and parts usage
Generate recurring work when maintenance is due
Update asset history and condition after each completed event

workflowProposals.name: Reserve study and capital plan utilization
workflowProposals.description: Stores reserve studies and turns them into actionable planning and monitoring inputs.
steps: Ingest reserve study documents and component data
Track lifecycle, useful life, and funding assumptions by component
Surface upcoming major repair or replacement needs
Connect planned capital work to budgets, decisions, and project execution
triggers: Component nearing end of useful life

workflowProposals.name: Internal case oversight and intervention
workflowProposals.description: Supports Hestami staff in supervising cases, exceptions, quality issues, and marketplace health.
steps: Queue cases requiring staff attention based on rules or manual assignment
Provide staff workspaces for outreach, provider coordination, and exception handling
Track intervention outcomes, quality issues, and unresolved blockers
Feed operational insights back into marketplace controls and reporting

workflowProposals.name: External service integration exchange
workflowProposals.description: Coordinates data exchange with third-party services needed to complete workflows.
steps: Send or receive data from maps, search, payments, messaging, accounting, or document systems
Normalize incoming external data into internal models
Handle failures, retries, and reconciliation of integration events
Expose connected outcomes back to the initiating workflow

workflowProposals.name: Operational KPI and reporting generation
workflowProposals.description: Produces dashboards, reports, and performance insights across properties, providers, and associations.
steps: Aggregate workflow, financial, compliance, and communication data
Calculate KPIs and trend metrics by persona context
Render dashboards, exception reports, and audit support outputs
Support drill-down from summary metrics to underlying records

integrationProposals.name: Identity Provider and SSO
integrationProposals.description: Authentication, MFA, passwordless login, enterprise SSO, and lifecycle controls for portal users, staff, vendors, and board members.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-2 and WF-19 by enforcing scoped access for portal accounts, association roles, and internal staff without building credential security in-house.

integrationProposals.name: Payment Gateway and Merchant Processing
integrationProposals.description: Card, ACH, wallet, and refund processing for invoices, assessments, fines, deposits, and on-site technician collections.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-8, WF-10, WF-17, and WF-19 for homeowner payments, association owner payments, and technician-collected field payments where regulated payment handling should remain with a specialist.

integrationProposals.name: Accounting and ERP Sync
integrationProposals.description: Synchronization of invoices, payments, ledger entries, AP bills, owner balances, and chart-of-accounts mappings with external accounting systems.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-17 and UJ-14 by connecting association accounting events to existing financial systems while preserving bidirectional status and reconciliation visibility.

integrationProposals.name: Bank Feed and Cash Reconciliation
integrationProposals.description: Pulls bank account transactions and balance data for reconciliation of assessments, AP disbursements, and operating cash activity.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-17 by reducing manual reconciliation effort for finance staff; bank systems remain authoritative so the platform primarily consumes transaction feeds.

integrationProposals.name: Maps, Geocoding, and Address Validation
integrationProposals.description: Address normalization, lat-long generation, map display, distance calculations, and service-area relevance checks.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-1, WF-6, and WF-9 by enriching property records, validating addresses, and enabling provider matching and technician navigation based on reliable geospatial data.

integrationProposals.name: Route Optimization and Dispatch Intelligence
integrationProposals.description: Travel-time estimation, stop sequencing, route optimization, and ETA updates for field appointments and technician assignments.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-9 and WF-10 by improving dispatch efficiency and appointment reliability for provider operators and technicians.

integrationProposals.name: SMS and Voice Messaging
integrationProposals.description: Transactional SMS, voice calls, reminders, delivery tracking, and opt-out handling for operational workflows.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-12, WF-9, and WF-20 for appointment reminders, notices, escalation outreach, and resident communications where carrier integrations are best outsourced.

integrationProposals.name: Email Delivery and Inbound Parsing
integrationProposals.description: Transactional email, inbound reply handling, bounce tracking, and template-based workflow communications.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-12, WF-18, and WF-20 for notices, board packets, vote-related correspondence, and case communications with auditable delivery outcomes.

integrationProposals.name: Postal Mail and Certified Notice Fulfillment
integrationProposals.description: Print-and-mail fulfillment, address validation, certified mail, and proof-of-mailing for legal or procedural notices.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-20 and WF-15 where violation notices, hearings, and certain association communications may require physical mailing and evidence of service.

integrationProposals.name: Unified Messaging Inbox
integrationProposals.description: Shared conversation inbox integration for staff-assisted support, provider follow-up, and omni-channel thread continuity.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-12, WF-23, and UJ-6 by allowing concierge staff to coordinate across channels while synchronizing conversation state back to internal case records.

integrationProposals.name: Object Storage and Secure File Delivery
integrationProposals.description: Durable storage for documents, photos, videos, signatures, board packets, and extracted artifacts with controlled access links.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-1, WF-10, and WF-11 by storing large media and document records securely and cost-effectively while application metadata remains internal.

integrationProposals.name: Document OCR and AI Extraction
integrationProposals.description: OCR, classification, field extraction, summarization, and confidence scoring for permits, insurance certificates, invoices, reserve studies, and governance records.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-11, WF-7, and WF-22 by turning uploaded or ingested documents into structured records faster than a fully owned extraction pipeline.

integrationProposals.name: Conversational AI and Reasoning Models
integrationProposals.description: Natural-language intake, triage assistance, summarization, comparison support, and draft generation for cases, messages, and decisions.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-4, WF-6, WF-14, and WF-23 by enabling AI-assisted intake, provider comparison, and evidence summarization without owning foundation model infrastructure.

integrationProposals.name: Web Search and Business Discovery
integrationProposals.description: Searches the public web for provider websites, contact details, business reputation signals, and rule or permit references relevant to a case.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6 and UJ-4 by broadening candidate provider discovery and external fact gathering during concierge-assisted and AI-assisted matching workflows.

integrationProposals.name: Business Reputation and Review Signals
integrationProposals.description: External ratings, review snippets, years-in-business indicators, and local business profile data to support provider trust signals.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-6 and WF-23 by enriching provider trust evaluation with third-party reputation evidence while keeping internal match rationale separate.

integrationProposals.name: Contractor License and Registration Verification
integrationProposals.description: Verification of contractor license status, registration status, expiration dates, and disciplinary indicators from official registries or aggregators.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-7 and WF-6 by validating vendor credentials during provider onboarding and pre-award compliance checks using external registries as the source of truth.

integrationProposals.name: Insurance Certificate Verification
integrationProposals.description: Validation of COI details, policy status, coverage limits, and named-insured information against insurer or verification services.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-7, WF-16, and WF-21 where associations and property operators need current proof of vendor insurance before work is scheduled or renewed.

integrationProposals.name: Permit and Municipal Lookup
integrationProposals.description: Lookup of permit requirements, filing status, jurisdictional rules, and municipal references tied to property work scopes.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-15, WF-20, and UJ-4 by surfacing permit requirements and municipal context before work begins or an ARC case is processed.

integrationProposals.name: Calendar Sync and Scheduling Interop
integrationProposals.description: Two-way synchronization of appointments, meeting invites, availability, and updates with staff, vendor, and board calendars.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-9 and WF-18 by keeping appointments and governance meetings aligned across external calendars while preserving platform workflow state.

integrationProposals.name: Electronic Signature
integrationProposals.description: Legally recognized e-sign flows for approvals, contracts, estimate acceptance, board consents, and acknowledgement forms.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-8, WF-10, and WF-18 by capturing enforceable approvals and acknowledgements without building signature compliance infrastructure.

integrationProposals.name: Video Meeting and Webinar Integration
integrationProposals.description: Virtual meeting links, attendance metadata, recordings, and scheduling support for board meetings, hearings, and staff-led coordination sessions.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-18 and WF-20 by enabling remote governance meetings and hearings while keeping formal decisions and minutes inside the platform.

integrationProposals.name: Sales Tax and Fee Calculation
integrationProposals.description: Jurisdiction-aware tax calculation for taxable invoice lines, materials, and service fees where applicable.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-8 for providers and marketplace billing scenarios where external tax engines reduce compliance risk across jurisdictions.

integrationProposals.name: Property Sensor and Device Telemetry
integrationProposals.description: Optional ingestion of leak sensors, thermostats, door access devices, or building telemetry to trigger maintenance awareness or evidence capture.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-1, WF-4, and WF-21 by enriching property context and maintenance workflows with external device signals when available.

integrationProposals.name: Smart Access and Entry Control
integrationProposals.description: Integration with lockboxes, gate systems, and smart locks for time-bounded technician access instructions and audit logs.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-9 and WF-10 by coordinating access windows and field-entry constraints for scheduled appointments and managed properties.

integrationProposals.name: Analytics Warehouse and BI Export
integrationProposals.description: Export of operational, financial, and marketplace data to external warehouses and BI tools for advanced reporting and portfolio analysis.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-25 and UJ-12 by enabling management companies and internal teams to combine platform data with broader reporting environments.

integrationProposals.name: CRM and Lifecycle Engagement
integrationProposals.description: Contact synchronization, lifecycle segmentation, service follow-up campaigns, and sales or account-management visibility.
integrationProposals.rationale: Supports WF-19 and WF-23 by connecting portal activity, marketplace engagement, and staff follow-up into broader customer relationship workflows.

qualityAttributes: All data reads and writes must enforce tenant-scoped authorization so that a user from one property, portfolio, association, or vendor organization cannot access another tenant's records in any API, job, export, or search result.

All portal and staff authentication flows must support MFA for privileged roles, and MFA enrollment must be required before any board member, finance user, or internal admin can approve payments, votes, or compliance outcomes.
All customer-managed and platform-managed files must be encrypted at rest with AES-256 or equivalent, and all network traffic must use TLS 1.2 or higher.

Every decision, approval, vote, payment status change, notice issuance, credential verification result, and workflow state transition must create an immutable audit event with actor, timestamp, prior state, new state, and linked evidence references.

The system must preserve exactly-once internal posting semantics for financial ledger events so that retries, webhook replays, or sync reruns cannot duplicate assessment, invoice, payment, or AP postings.
For 95 percent of interactive requests under normal operating load, authenticated web and mobile API responses must complete within 2 seconds, excluding large file uploads and report exports.

The mobile field workflow must allow technicians to create notes, photos, time entries, signatures, and completion events offline for at least 24 hours and must automatically reconcile queued changes when connectivity returns.

Outbound email, SMS, and postal notice workflows must persist delivery status, failure reason, and recipient endpoint for every delivery attempt within 60 seconds of provider callback or sync receipt.
Role and permission changes must take effect for new requests within 60 seconds, and terminated or revoked users must lose access to active sessions within 15 minutes or less.
The platform must support point-in-time recovery for primary transactional data with a recovery point objective of 15 minutes and a recovery time objective of 4 hours.
Document extraction results must retain provenance linking each extracted field to the source document, page or region when available, extraction model version, and confidence score.
All provider compliance checks must record the external verification source, verification timestamp, normalized status, and expiration date so that stale credentials can be identified automatically.

The system must maintain 99.9 percent monthly availability for core portal, case management, and payment initiation functions, excluding scheduled maintenance windows announced at least 48 hours in advance.
Search, exports, analytics snapshots, and background automations must respect the same row-level access controls as transactional screens and APIs.
All legally significant notices, violation events, hearing schedules, and board vote records must be retained for at least 7 years unless a stricter tenant retention policy is configured.
PII, payment tokens, signatures, and credential documents must be classified and access-logged, and direct access to their raw contents must be restricted to explicitly authorized roles.
Workflow timers and SLA escalations must continue to execute within 5 minutes of scheduled time even during partial third-party outages, using durable internal queues and retry policies.
Every external integration webhook and sync job must be idempotent, traceable, and replayable, with a stored correlation identifier and final status for each run.
Generated PDFs, portal pages, and core user workflows must conform to WCAG 2.1 AA for keyboard navigation, contrast, labeling, and screen-reader compatibility.

The platform must provide tenant-configurable data retention and deletion policies for messages, documents, sessions, and analytics outputs while preventing deletion of records subject to legal hold or financial audit requirements.

All AI-generated triage, extraction, match, and summary outputs shown to users must display a confidence or uncertainty indicator and must never overwrite previously stored user-entered facts without an explicit confirmation event.

Financial reports and exported ledgers must be reproducible from retained source transactions so that the same report definition and as-of date produce materially identical results when rerun against an unchanged ledger.
Provider matching and trust scoring must log the factors used to rank each recommended provider so staff or customers can review the rationale behind the recommendation.

Background jobs handling billing, notices, extraction, or sync must expose metrics, structured logs, and alertable failure states so operators can identify stuck or repeatedly failing workflows within 15 minutes.
uxRequirements: The product must minimize effort for both property owners and service providers, reflecting the stated vision of reducing friction on both sides.
Property context should be persistent and reusable so users do not need to repeatedly reconstruct the same information.
User experiences should be role-based because the platform explicitly serves owners, tenants, managers, vendors, board members, staff, and admins.
Workflows should preserve transparency and trust through visible decision history, supporting evidence, and status traceability.
Mobile experiences should support task-centric workflows for homeowners and technicians.
Field and mobile workflows should tolerate low-connectivity conditions where explicitly required for technicians.
Documents, media, and communications should be attached to the relevant property, case, unit, or stakeholder context rather than existing as isolated artifacts.
Portal and operational experiences should support dense information display for administrative and management users while remaining approachable for homeowners and residents.

requirements.text: The platform must support three product pillars: Hestami Home Real Property Assistant, Hestami Service Provider Field Services Management, and Hestami Community Association Management.
Phase 1 must support property owners adding or updating properties with address and location information and uploading photos or videos.
Phase 1 must support homeowners creating a service call case.
The product must support AI agent or Hestami staff facilitation of service-provider discovery and matching through internet searches.

The Hestami Home Real Property Assistant must cover the nine stated business domains including property and ownership modeling, portfolio management, owner intent intake, service call lifecycle, property document management, human staff execution, external HOA context tracking, external vendor coordination, and decision/rationale trust ledger.

The platform must preserve a logical common data model spanning associations, properties, people, ownership or tenancy relationships, vendors, work orders, documents, communications, governance, accounting, reserve studies, compliance, and owner portal roles.

The community association management pillar must support association or property modeling, accounting, work orders and vendor management, violations, ARC requests, governance and meetings, communications, owner portal capabilities, documents and records, reserve studies, and compliance.
Accounting capabilities must include an auditable and compliant general ledger structure, automatic periodic assessment posting, and integration from work orders to accounts payable to payments.
Workflow automation must support tasks, triggers, SLA timers, notifications, and approvals.
Violation workflows must support evidence, notice sequences, hearings, and fines tied to financial records.
ARC workflows must support requests, plans or documents, review actions, conditions, and permit-related context.
Work-order and maintenance workflows must support work orders, assets, vendors, bids or proposals, schedules, completion, and invoice-to-AP workflow.
Communication capabilities must support mass email, SMS, announcements, letter templates, calendar events, and meeting notices.
Governance capabilities must support boards, board roles, meetings, agendas, minutes, voting, resolutions, and policy documents.
Owner portal or CRM capabilities must support user accounts, role-based access for owner, tenant, manager, and vendor, requests, payment preferences, and document access.

Documents and records capabilities must support governing documents, financial reports, contracts, reserve studies, ARC plans, and inspection reports, with AI-driven extraction, classification, and summarization as core enhancement features.
Compliance capabilities must support statutory deadlines, notice requirements, voting rules, financial audit requirements, and resale packet guidelines.

The service provider field services management pillar must support contractor identity and compliance, workforce and technician management, pricebook and materials, job lifecycle, dispatch and scheduling, technician mobile operations, estimates and invoicing, inventory and procurement, and maintenance contracts.
Technician mobile operations must support job details, documentation capture, time tracking, on-site estimate generation, payment acceptance, and offline operation.

The product must support role-specific portals or interfaces for admins, staff, property owners, service providers, management-company managers and staff, and board members, with native mobile clients for homeowners and technicians.

decisions.text: The product scope is intentionally organized into three major pillars that together define the platform: owner-side assistant, service-provider FSM, and community association management.
Release sequencing at the source-document level is owner assistant first, service-provider FSM second, and community association management third.
The owner-side assistant is positioned around minimizing friction in matching property owners and service providers through digital asset exchange and persistent property context.
Human-assisted execution is part of the product, not just AI automation; the Home Real Property Assistant explicitly includes a Human Hestami Staff Service Call Execution domain.
The Home Real Property Assistant includes support for external vendor coordination without requiring provider onboarding.
The Home Real Property Assistant includes external HOA context tracking when the HOA is not a platform participant.
A durable decision, rationale, and trust ledger is a core product concept for the owner-side experience.
ServiceTitan is the stated reference model for the service-provider FSM pillar, implying an operating-system approach for trade businesses rather than a narrow job board or lead-gen product.

Vantaca is the stated reference model for the community association management pillar, implying an all-in-one system of action with role-based portals, workflows, and auditable records for association operations.
AI-driven document extraction, classification, and summarization are intended as core enhancement features for documents and records rather than optional add-ons.

constraints.text: External integrations with potential partners like Nextdoor are expected but explicitly out of scope at this time.
The product must accommodate long-lived property and governance history, implying that records must remain persistent even as owners and managers change over time.
The platform operates in domains that require auditable records, including accounting, governance, compliance, and decision history.
Compliance-related requirements are significant in the CAM pillar, including statutory deadlines, notice requirements, voting rules, audit requirements, and resale packet guidelines.
Technician-facing mobile workflows must be robust in low-connectivity environments.

The initial source materials do not provide detailed product definition for Pillar 2 and Pillar 3 beyond domain-level scope and reference examples, so their feature boundaries remain only partially specified.
The stated success criterion is holistic and complete implementation of each pillar, indicating broad strategic scope but not providing operational launch metrics or narrower release thresholds.

openQuestions.text: Within Phase 1, which customer segment is the primary launch focus: individual homeowners, small landlords, or property investors managing multiple residences?
What is the intended business model in Phase 1: software subscription, concierge service fee, lead or referral fee, transaction fee, or a hybrid model?
How much of provider discovery and matching should be automated by AI versus reviewed or operated by Hestami staff at launch?

What level of provider verification is required in Phase 1 before a provider can be surfaced to owners: basic public discovery only, license and insurance verification, or deeper business legitimacy checks?
Should Phase 1 include structured bid comparison and estimate normalization for owners, given that inconsistent and incomplete bids are listed as a core problem?

What owner-facing outcomes are in scope for Phase 1 after provider matching: shortlist only, scheduling assistance, bid management, end-to-end job coordination, or post-job warranty and feedback handling?
Which property document types are mandatory in the initial owner experience: permits, governing documents, approvals, appliance records, correspondence, or all of these?

How deep should the digital-property-context vision go in the first release: simple media upload only, or also floorplan generation, appliance or equipment inventory, and advanced representations such as 3D Gaussian Splats?
When the spec references government-service integration for permits, licensing, and related checks, are those intended as near-term product commitments or future-direction ideas?
For Phase 1, should service providers be required to onboard into Hestami at all, or is external vendor coordination without onboarding the default operating model?
Which trade categories should be supported first for owner requests and provider matching, such as lawn maintenance, plumbing, HVAC, roofing, or a broader set?
What are the explicit boundaries between Phase 1 owner-side workflows and Phase 2 contractor workflows so the initial release does not expand prematurely into full FSM complexity?
When using ServiceTitan as a model for Pillar 2, which capabilities are strategically essential to mirror and which are intentionally out of scope for Hestami's first contractor release?
When using Vantaca as a model for Pillar 3, is the target buyer the self-managed association, the professional management company, or both?
Should community association management remain entirely Phase 3, or are there Phase 1 or Phase 2 owner-facing HOA workflows that must ship earlier because they materially affect service-call success?
What measurable launch success metrics should replace the current high-level statement of complete and correct implementation, especially for Phase 1?
What geographic market or regulatory jurisdiction is the first target, since licensing, permits, statutory rules, and HOA practices vary materially by location?
What level of resident and tenant self-service is expected in the owner portal and CAM portal at initial launch versus later expansion?

openLoops.description: Confirm the primary Phase 1 launch segment among homeowners, small landlords, and property investors so onboarding, messaging, and scope can be narrowed appropriately.
Define the Phase 1 commercial model, including whether revenue is driven by software subscription, concierge fees, referral or lead fees, transaction fees, or a hybrid structure.
Set the launch operating model for provider discovery and matching, including the boundary between AI automation and Hestami staff review or intervention.
Decide the minimum provider verification bar required before surfacing vendors, including whether license, insurance, and deeper legitimacy checks are mandatory at launch.

Lock the Phase 1 owner journey boundary after matching, including whether the release stops at shortlist generation or extends into scheduling, bid comparison, coordination, and post-job follow-through.

Clarify the first-release depth of digital property context, including mandatory document types and whether advanced representations such as equipment inventory, floorplans, or richer spatial models are in scope.
Choose the initial trade categories and geographic market so provider networks, verification rules, permit assumptions, and regulatory constraints can be operationalized.
Define the explicit boundary between Phase 1 owner workflows and later contractor FSM capabilities to avoid premature expansion into full operational complexity.
Determine whether providers must onboard into Hestami in Phase 1 or whether external vendor coordination is the default operating model.
Resolve whether owner-facing HOA and governance context needs to appear before the full association-management phase because it materially affects maintenance and service outcomes.
Replace high-level implementation goals with explicit Phase 1 launch success metrics tied to adoption, service outcomes, trust, and operational efficiency.
Set the initial level of resident and tenant self-service across owner and association portals so role boundaries and portal scope remain controlled.
